description: Refutation fixture: spec cites a fabricated ULC designation ('CAN/ULC-S199') as the fire-door test standard; verifier correctly DISPUTED it, and the intended replacement is uncertain, so a human must resolve.

finding.issue: Specification cites 'CAN/ULC-S199' as the fire-door test standard; no such ULC standard exists.

paragraph_map_slice.text: A. Fire door assemblies: tested per CAN/ULC-S199.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: There is no CAN/ULC-S199. The Canadian fire-door test standard is CAN/ULC-S104 (Fire Tests of Door Assemblies), and frames are covered by CAN/ULC-S105 — but depending on what the author meant (door assembly vs. frame vs. glazing, which would be CAN/ULC-S106) the single replacement cannot be asserted with confidence. The designation is fabricated and must be corrected by a human.

ground_truth.notes: Fabricated designation in the S-series pattern the field trial warned about. Refutation is where verification earns its cost; the model's single replacement is uncertain, so DISPUTED (not CORRECTED) is right.
